info.title: Postmark Service API
info.description: Email sending and tracking service built on Postmark. Handles email delivery via the broadcast message stream, webhook processing for delivery events, and integrates with a runs-service for cost tracking.

audienceId.description: Audience ID for cost attribution. Header x-audience-id takes precedence.

leadId.description: Lead ID for tracking and dedup

from.description: Sender email address. If omitted, resolved from key-service (provider: postmark-from-address).

contacted.description: A sending exists for this message

sent.description: Sending accepted by Postmark (errorCode=0), or implied by downstream events

delivered.description: Delivery webhook received, or implied by open/click. False if bounced

opened.description: Open webhook received, or implied by click

replied.description: Always false — Postmark has no reply tracking

replyClassification.description: Always null — Postmark has no reply tracking

email.description: The email address that was looked up

contacted.description: Whether at least one sending exists in this scope

sent.description: Whether an email was sent in this scope

delivered.description: Whether an email was delivered in this scope (sent AND not bounced)

opened.description: Whether the recipient opened any email in this scope

clicked.description: Whether the recipient clicked any link in this scope

replied.description: Whether the recipient replied in this scope (always false for postmark — no reply tracking)

replyClassification.description: Classification of most recent reply: positive / negative / neutral, or null if no reply (always null for postmark)

bounced.description: Whether an email bounced in this scope

unsubscribed.description: Whether the recipient unsubscribed in this scope

cancelled.description: Whether the campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job. Optional in contract v1; required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

lastDeliveredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last delivery in this scope; null if none

firstContactedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a contacted event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstSentAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a sent event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstDeliveredAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a delivered event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstOpenedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of an opened event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstClickedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a clicked event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstRepliedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a replied event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope (always null for postmark — no reply tracking)

firstBouncedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a bounced event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstUnsubscribedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of an unsubscribed event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

byCampaign.description: Per-campaign breakdown (brand mode only). Keys are campaign UUIDs, values are scoped status. Null in campaign and global modes.

campaign.description: Status scoped to the requested campaignId (campaign mode only). Null in brand and global modes.

bounced.description: True if this email bounced anywhere in the org

unsubscribed.description: True if this email unsubscribed anywhere in the org

global.description: Org-wide email health flags. Always present in all modes.

brandId.description: Brand UUID(s), comma-separated — activates brand mode: returns byCampaign breakdown + aggregated brand scope. Ignored if campaignId is also provided.

campaignId.description: Campaign UUID — activates campaign mode: returns campaign-scoped status. Takes precedence over brandId.

email.description: Recipient email address to look up

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

sourceBrandId.description: Brand UUID to transfer from the source org

sourceOrgId.description: Current org UUID that owns the brand

targetOrgId.description: Target org UUID to transfer the brand to

targetBrandId.description: Brand UUID in the target org to rewrite to (when target org already has a brand for the same domain)

post.description: Send an email via Postmark and record it in the database. Runs-service integration is BLOCKING.

schema.description: Brand ID(s), comma-separated for multi-brand campaigns (injected by workflow-service). Example: uuid1,uuid2,uuid3

parameters.description: Brand ID(s), comma-separated for multi-brand campaigns (injected by workflow-service). Example: uuid1,uuid2,uuid3
parameters.name: x-brand-id

post.description: Send up to 500 emails in one request. Runs-service integration is BLOCKING for each email.

schema.description: Brand ID(s), comma-separated for multi-brand campaigns (injected by workflow-service). Example: uuid1,uuid2,uuid3

parameters.description: Brand ID(s), comma-separated for multi-brand campaigns (injected by workflow-service). Example: uuid1,uuid2,uuid3
parameters.name: x-brand-id

get.description: Get the full delivery status of an email by its Postmark message ID
Get recent emails for an organization

schema.description: Max results to return. Omit to return all.

parameters.description: Max results to return. Omit to return all.
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Get all emails for a specific run

post.description:
Check delivery status for a batch of email addresses. The response shape depends on the body filters:

| brandId | campaignId | Mode     | Active fields                  |
|---------|------------|----------|--------------------------------|
| absent  | absent     | Global   | global                         |
| present | absent     | Brand    | byCampaign + brand + global    |
| absent  | present    | Campaign | campaign + global              |
| present | present    | Campaign | campaign + global (brandId ignored) |

Non-applicable fields are null. Headers (x-brand-id, x-campaign-id, etc.) are for tracing/logging only — they do NOT drive filtering logic. Filters are in the request body.

Aggregation rules:
- byCampaign[id].*: exact status for that campaign
- brand.*: BOOL_OR across all campaigns of the brand (true if true in any campaign)
- brand.lastDeliveredAt: MAX across all campaigns
- global.email.bounced/unsubscribed: aggregated across all campaigns in the org (no brand/campaign filter)

200.description: Per-item status results. Shape depends on mode — see description.

get.description: Get aggregated email stats optionally filtered by runIds, orgId, brandId, campaignId, workflowSlugs, and/or featureSlugs. When groupBy is provided, returns grouped results. Requires x-org-id header.

schema.description: Filter by brand ID (comma-separated for multiple)

parameters.description: Filter by brand ID (comma-separated for multiple)
parameters.name: brandId

200.description: Aggregated stats (flat or grouped depending on groupBy parameter)

get.description: Same as GET /orgs/stats but only requires X-API-Key (no identity headers). Used by email-gateway for transactional stats aggregation.

schema.description: Filter by brand ID (comma-separated for multiple)

parameters.description: Filter by brand ID (comma-separated for multiple)
parameters.name: brandId

200.description: Aggregated stats (flat or grouped depending on groupBy parameter)

get.description: Returns global workflow performance stats. API key only, no identity headers required.

post.description: Re-assigns solo-brand postmark_sendings rows from sourceOrgId to targetOrgId. Skips co-branding rows (multiple brand IDs). Idempotent.

get.description: Returns the webhook URL that BYOK users should configure in their Postmark dashboard, along with the list of event types to enable.

post.description: Public Postmark webhook endpoint. Persists Delivery, Bounce, Open, Click, SpamComplaint, SubscriptionChange events to local storage. RecordType=Inbound is forwarded byte-for-byte to email-gateway at ${EMAIL_GATEWAY_SERVICE_URL}/inbound/postmark with x-api-key auth; non-2xx or network errors return 502 so Postmark's own retry kicks in (no outbox, no queue).

502.description: Inbound forward to email-gateway failed (non-2xx response or network error). Postmark will retry.
